# TASK_BRIEF_2026-07-01_p0-05-performance-feedback.md
# Game: Send in the Clowns | Codename: clowns | Phase: 0 (solo combat toy)
# Generated by: technical_designer_agent (Mission Control)
# Route: Cursor + Unity MCP (VFX/audio/animation hooks) + Claude Code CLI (C#)
# Status: ACTIVE | Depends on: TB-04
# Format: conforms to intent of CLAUDE_PROJECT_RULES.md §7.2.

## 0. Objective
Make every cast **read** as landing or bombing — entirely through performance (Pillar 3), with
**zero written text** — using grey-box primitives driven off the result events from TB-03/04, so
the "did the bit work?" signal is instant and legible before any real art exists.

## 1. Why this brief locks NO design choice
- Feedback is described by a `PerformanceSignatureSO` per humor type: `float preDelay` (cadence
  wind-up), `float landPunchScale`, `Color landFlash`, `AudioClip landBlip`, `Color bombDesat`,
  `AudioClip bombBlip`, `float bombFizzleDuration`. These are the SCOPEABLE cadence/action signature
  from `HUMOR_SYSTEM.md` §6 — as data, tunable by ear/eye, no recompile.
- It commits to NO final VFX/animation. Grey-box primitives now; real signatures are a
  narrative_agent (cadence) + audio task later. Swapping in real assets = fill the same SO fields.
- Whether the game ends up with 3 or 5 humor types doesn't matter: one signature asset per type,
  added as data.

## 2. Co-op-forward seams honored
- **Seam 6:** feedback subscribes to `CastStartedEvent` (wind-up) and `MirthResultEvent` (land/bomb).
  It reads events; it holds NO game logic. On a remote client later, replaying the broadcast event
  reproduces the exact beat — no rework.
- Feedback is view-only and owner-agnostic: it plays for any actor's cast, local or (future) remote.

## 3. Prerequisites
- [ ] TB-04 complete (casts fire; `CastStartedEvent` + `MirthResultEvent` are raised).

## 4. Files to create
`Assets/_clowns/Runtime/Feedback/`:
- `PerformanceSignatureSO.cs` — the fields in §1. One asset per humor type. (`HumorTypeSO` gets a
  nullable `PerformanceSignatureSO signature` back-ref, or map via a small `SignatureBookSO` lookup —
  pick the lookup to avoid a circular ref; record choice in a comment.)
- `CastPerformer.cs` — `MonoBehaviour` on the clown. On `CastStartedEvent` for its caster: plays the
  `preDelay` cadence beat (a scale/pose "wind-up" on the clown primitive). Purely the delivery pose.
- `TargetReactor.cs` — `MonoBehaviour` on the enemy. On `MirthResultEvent` for its target:
  - **Land** (grade Strong/Neutral, not bombed): punch-scale the enemy up (`landPunchScale`), flash
    `landFlash` on its mesh + mirth bar, play `landBlip`, emit an abstract non-text sparkle particle.
    Bigger grade = bigger punch (Strong reads clearly stronger than Neutral).
  - **Bomb** (`bombed==true`): desaturate to `bombDesat`, a small downward "shrug" scale, play
    `bombBlip` (placeholder sad-trombone stand-in), grey fizzle particle, brief `bombFizzleDuration`.
- `MirthBarView.cs` — the world-space bar (from TB-03) animates fill; on land, a quick colour pop;
  on bomb, a grey flash. NUMBERS optional, NO text labels.

`Assets/_clowns/GameData/`:
- `PS_Wit`, `PS_Slapstick`, `PS_Dark` signature assets. Seed distinct grey-box dials so the three are
  tellable blind (per `HUMOR_SYSTEM` §6 spirit): e.g. Wit = short `preDelay`, crisp high `landBlip`,
  small tight punch; Slapstick = medium wind-up, low "splat" `landBlip`, big bouncy punch; Dark = long
  `preDelay`, low flat `landBlip`, minimal punch + a colour shift. (Placeholders — tunable.)
- `SignatureBook_Toy` (SignatureBookSO) mapping the three HumorTypeSOs → their signatures.

## 5. Ordered steps
1. **[CLI]** Author `PerformanceSignatureSO`, `SignatureBookSO`, `CastPerformer`, `TargetReactor`,
   `MirthBarView` (namespace `Clowns.Feedback`).
2. **[Cursor]** Add `CastPerformer` to the clown prefab, `TargetReactor` + `MirthBarView` to the enemy.
3. **[CLI]** Create the three `PS_*` assets + `SignatureBook_Toy`; wire the book on the reactor.
4. **[Cursor]** Add two simple `ParticleSystem` prefabs: `FX_Land_Sparkle` (bright, abstract) and
   `FX_Bomb_Fizzle` (grey, downward). NO text/emoji particles.
5. **[CLI]** Add placeholder `AudioClip`s (or a `AudioSource.pitch` shift on a single blip if no clips
   yet) so each type's land/bomb is audibly distinct.
6. **[CLI]** `git commit -m "Phase 0 TB-05: text-free land/bomb performance feedback (grey-box, event-driven)"`.

## 6. Guardrails (restate — the sharpest one for this brief)
- **ABSOLUTELY no written jokes/punchlines/comedy text** in particles, UI, audio filenames-as-copy,
  or on-screen strings (`DEC-2026-06-29-C`, Pillar 3). The signal is cadence + motion + colour + sound.
  A blindfolded player should still tell Wit from Slapstick by sound; eyes-only by silhouette/motion.
- Feedback must be driven by events only — if `TargetReactor` ever calls back into `MirthMeter` logic,
  reject (breaks the co-op replay seam).
- Grey-box only: no custom shaders (scope debt per technical-designer flags), no final art.

## 7. Verification (USER confirms) — Pillar 3 gate in miniature
- [ ] Every cast produces an immediate, readable land OR bomb beat; the player always knows which.
- [ ] Strong reads visibly bigger than Neutral; a bomb reads clearly as a bomb (down, grey, fizzle).
- [ ] With the screen muted, the three types are distinguishable by motion; with eyes closed,
      distinguishable by sound. (Rough is fine — grey-box.)
- [ ] There is NO text anywhere conveying the joke or its result.

## 8. Failure modes → diagnosis → fix
- *Land/bomb feels mushy or late* → shorten `preDelay`/tighten punch; it's data.
- *Types feel samey* → widen the dial spread across `PS_*` (pitch, wind-up, punch size).
- *Circular asmdef/ref between HumorType and Signature* → use the `SignatureBookSO` lookup, not a
  back-ref field.
- *Feedback double-fires* → ensure `TargetReactor` filters events by its own `targetId`.

## 9. Exit gate (binary)
Every cast lands or bombs with an instant, text-free, event-driven performance beat that distinguishes
grade and humor type. → unlocks TB-06.

## 10. Out of scope for this brief
Real animation/audio authoring (later, narrative_agent + audio), temperament tells (TB-06), spawning
& loss (TB-06), the second axis / Heart type (Layer A), any written content ever.
